title: loheria crassifolia
intro: Loheria crassifolia, also known as the Malabar ironwood or the loheria tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the tropical rainforests of Sri Lanka and is traditionally valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Loheria crassifolia tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
